scope: Repair the public Baton vendor boundary so exact v0.15.1 candidates are materialised and validated before mutation, VERSION participates in the same durable transaction, recovery fails closed, check mode is mutation-free, valid results map only to public exits 0, 1, or 2, and the stable committed semantic diff receives its exact planning-authority Implementer maintainability preflight before handoff.

reachability.evidence: bin/sworn baton vendor /home/brad/projects/baton --check exited 1 and printed only a deterministic 17-path changed list. This drives the built public command against the exact Baton v0.15.1 checkout while proving valid drift reaches exit 1 without exposing mapped payload bytes. cmd/sworn/baton_test.go:TestBatonVendorAtomicPreflightReachability additionally drives clean, drift, invalid-source, apply-failure, incomplete-rollback, recovery-only, positional --check, upstream-before-network, VERSION-drift, and mode-only cases through the public command boundary.

delivered.item: AC-01: lexical script detection accepts board.json.shared_touchpoints prose while rejecting exact unmapped shell, Python, and module-script tokens before write.
AC-02: the untouched v0.15 board schema compiles through the bounded ECMA-pattern adapter and preserves all required path and line-terminator semantics.

AC-03: one invocation instant constructs VERSION before mutation; mapped files and VERSION share ordered snapshot, atomic replacement, rollback, verification, and Git-admin-confined recovery authority that rejects untrusted material before ordinary writes.

AC-04: the built public command maps clean or successful write to 0, valid byte drift to 1, and all invalid or operational paths to 2; check mode performs full materialisation without worktree, VERSION, mode, or Git-admin mutation and diagnostics remain path-only.
delivered.evidence: cmd/sworn/baton.go; cmd/sworn/baton_test.go:TestBatonVendorAtomicPreflightReachability; live CLI reachability evidence in this proof

delivered.item: AC-05 Implementer half: the planning-authority adapter constructed the exact tagged v0.15.1 committed semantic scope across the recognized release-wt synchronizations, invoked one fresh temperature-0 role-isolated review, persisted its schema-valid cycle-0 preflight PASS, pinned the committed report blob and input fingerprint in the append-only ledger, and set maintainability.implementation_head to the reviewed semantic head. The distinct fresh Verifier authoritative review remains mandatory before verified.
delivered.evidence: docs/release/2026-07-15-baton-v0.15-conformance/S01-vendor-boundary-readiness/reports/maintainability/implementer-cycle-0-121966a1-0de7-43c5-bd65-fcb901f0ebe6.json at blob 19bfd61e8b82e616df3e657f5b783918948b25ca; status.json maintainability report ledger; review_scope.head d062d055cdbe90e8290f0bf47574be660cd9a675; input_fingerprint sha256:6dccd1e4ddaa76e9622f86026fa62700dc935b6ac5b65df95191974829a8bbad

delivered.item: The transaction orders destination paths with an O(total bytes plus files) byte-radix pass and discovers the complete candidate set from the mapping, so later S02 mapping additions do not require another vendor ordering authority.

not_delivered.item: Execute the exact v0.15.1 content and VERSION pin update and refresh the local Codex and Claude Baton mirrors.
not_delivered.why: The Coach-ratified boundary keeps S01 limited to transaction machinery and proof so content replacement occurs only after this bootstrap boundary is independently verified.

not_delivered.item: Deliver the generalized public maintainability command, automated lifecycle coordinator, track-composition and rollback handling, bootstrap-report revalidation, and planning-to-current cutover.
not_delivered.why: S01 executes only the exact per-slice planning-authority adapter required for its own Gate-8 evidence; it grants no reusable public command or current protocol authority.

divergence.description: The implementation pre-publishes the complete owner-only recovery authority before the first replacement, rather than creating it only after an incomplete rollback, so a process crash during apply remains recoverable under the same normative authority.

Successful recovery authority is retired by atomically renaming the complete fixed root out of authority; a later write invocation scrubs deterministic retired or staging debris and returns exit 2 for a clean rerun after performing recovery maintenance only.
Deterministic destination order uses an MSD byte-radix pass instead of comparison sorting, preserving the slice's linear-complexity contract without introducing a second hardcoded path list.
